articles.description: The Nationalist Citizens Party of India asserted its recognized status by the Election Commission. Opposition parties staged a walkout during an all-party meeting over invited rebel MPs. Sudip Bandyopadhyay stated the NCPI is the largest constituent of the ruling NDA. He also demanded that smaller parties should not receive front-row seats. The Lok Sabha Speaker will decide the status of the 20 rebel TMC lawmakers.

articles.title: Karnataka CM puts officials on drought alert, announces Rs 1 crore aid for each taluk
articles.description: Karnataka officials are directed to work continuously for drought preparedness. Immediate funds are allocated for drinking water supply across all taluks. Farmers must not suffer due to administrative negligence or apathy. Drinking water, fodder, and crop insurance are prioritized for rural areas. The government will verify field assessments against official reports.

articles.title: Space Exploration Day | How are astronauts trained?
articles.description: If you want to be in space, you’ll need training. A lot of it. Here’s a look at all the different training — from spacewalk to simulation — that astronauts undergo to be efficient and valiant when, after much waiting, they finally get to space!

articles.title: 'Govt afraid of Sonam Wangchuk's march': Angmo
Why Samsung’s passport-style foldable phone matters for the smartphone industry
Government launches White Rabbit tech network to secure Indian Standard Time
articles.description: The system disseminates Indian Standard Time, traceable to the national time-keeping authority UTC (NPLI), using Precision Time Protocol-based White Rabbit technology, which the ministry said in a statement, offers highly accurate synchronisation for sectors, including banking, telecommunications, power grids, transportation, and digital governance.

articles.title: San Francisco wants Google and Apple to remove AI ‘Nudification’ apps from their app stores
articles.description: Apple and Google both reiterated their stance on policy violating-apps and confirmed they had taken action against the offending apps

articles.title: U.S. judge won't block Meta from laying off workers who filed AI discrimination lawsuit
articles.description: The judge said the workers could not show that losing their jobs amounted to the “irreparable harm” required for him to issue an emergency order blocking the layoffs

articles.title: Apple unseats Nvidia to become world's most valuable company as AI bets shift
articles.description: Apple was last ​valued at $4.88 trillion as its shares held steady, while Nvidia was roughly at $4.86 trillion, ⁠following a 3.5% decline

articles.title: US renews strikes on Iran after troops killed
Punjab govt releases over Rs 1,400 crore under old-age pension scheme: Minister
articles.description: Punjab's social security minister announced over Rs 1,423 crore disbursed for the old-age pension scheme. Approximately 23.77 lakh senior citizens across the state are currently receiving financial aid. The government has allocated Rs 4,000 crore for this welfare program this financial year. Officials are directed to ensure timely and transparent processing of all eligible beneficiaries. Permanent residents meeting income and age criteria can avail this important social protection benefit.

articles.title: Income-tax, MSME Bills in Monsoon Session
Pinarayi Vijayan extends support to students on hunger strike in Delhi
articles.description: Pinarayi Vijayan voiced support for students on hunger strike in New Delhi. He stated the nation's youth will not yield to the current government's authoritarian rule. Student leaders are protesting alleged irregularities in competitive examinations, including the NEET paper leak. They are demanding accountability and the resignation of the Union Education Minister. Vijayan emphasized listening to students' dreams and addressing their concerns for India's future.

articles.title: Humerus: On July 17, 2026

Nouriel Roubini: the world faces its worst ever energy disruption but seems reluctant to fully price it in
articles.description: While a lot has changed since the oil shocks of the 1970s to reduce their damage, a re-eruption of full-scale war in West Asia between the US and Iran can’t be shrugged off. Markets don’t seem to have priced in the tail risks yet.

articles.title: Goa BJP MLA, ex-minister Jennifer Monserrate passes away
articles.description: Goa BJP MLA and former minister Jennifer Monserrate passed away on Sunday. She was 56 years old and represented the Taleigao constituency. Monserrate was a three-term legislator who joined the BJP in 2019. Chief Minister Pramod Sawant expressed his deep sorrow over her demise. He offered condolences to her family and well-wishers.

articles.title: PLI success prompts Centre to explore next phase for food processing sector
articles.description: The food processing ministry is reviewing lessons from the current PLI programme and consulting industry stakeholders before finalizing a new framework. It has also held discussions with PLI beneficiaries to identify segments requiring additional policy support.

articles.title: 'They are using Sonam Wangchuk': Woman who threw ink at Abhijeet Dipke alleges she was assaulted by "goons"
articles.description: Being a self-described men's rights activist, Trehan said she has no political affiliation and alleged that the protest at Jantar Mantar had nothing to do with the NEET controversy but had a "political agenda", accusing the demonstration of using activist Sonam Wangchuk.

articles.title: What is context bombing, a new AI defence technique turning hackers’ tricks against them?
Calcutta High Court orders halt in demolition of Abhishek Banerjee's office
articles.description: The Calcutta High Court has ordered a halt to the demolition of Abhishek Banerjee's constituency office. The court directed the state government to submit all relevant documents concerning the Amtala building. This building is reportedly owned by 'Leaps and Bounds' Company, where Banerjee is the CEO. The company had challenged the demolition order before the high court.

articles.title: "Completely bogus, fake news": Jairam Ramesh denies reports of support for Delimitation Bill at all-party meeting
articles.description: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh denied reports of Delimitation Bill support at an all-party meeting. Opposition parties staged a brief walkout from the meeting protesting an invitation. They objected to the National Citizens Party of India's participation in the discussions. Opposition parties also stressed the need for prior discussions on key bills. The Monsoon Session of Parliament is scheduled to commence soon.

articles.title: Watch out: OpenAI's new AI speaker may be designed to fit a hole in the user’s heart
articles.description: In a market that has Amazon's Alexa and Google's Gemini for Home, an OpenAI device designed by ex-Apple Jony Ive will face stiff competition. But while others aim to be useful, this one will likely try to get users emotionally hooked. Does this bend the ethics of addictive-by-design tech?

articles.title: 5paisa Capital partners with OpenAI to bring AI-powered investing in India
articles.description: 5paisa Capital has partnered with OpenAI to integrate AI-powered investing and trading features across its platform. The collaboration will offer personalised insights, portfolio analysis and decision support, with plans to make 5paisa's services accessible within ChatGPT through conversational AI.

articles.title: Govt defends invitation to rebel TMC MPs for all-party meet
articles.description: The government defended inviting rebel TMC MPs to an all-party meeting. These MPs petitioned the Lok Sabha Speaker for recognition and separate seats. The minister urged all parties to support bills for Parliament's smooth functioning. The Monsoon session begins Monday and continues until August thirteenth.

articles.title: Sanjay Singh moves Rule 267 notice to discuss NEET paper leak, Sonam Wangchuk's "declining health"
articles.description: Aam Aadmi Party MP Sanjay Singh has moved a notice for Rajya Sabha adjournment. He seeks discussion on the NEET-UG paper leak and related student issues. The notice also highlights the deteriorating health of activist Sonam Wangchuk. Singh calls for a discussion on these matters of national importance. The Monsoon Session of Parliament is scheduled to begin on Monday.

articles.title: Afghanistan seeks deeper trade ties with India, pitches for easier business access
articles.description: India has also been stepping up engagement with Kabul, with Afghan Agriculture Minister Mawlawi Ataullah Omari visiting New Delhi earlier this month.

articles.title: Why we cannot abandon GDP and what the world can do to improve its utility as a statistical gauge
articles.description: Gross domestic product (GDP) has been long criticized for its inadequacy in measuring economic activity or welfare. True, it is imperfect. But it is too deeply embedded in the institutional architecture of governments, markets and global organizations to drop.

articles.title: Accenture tweaks salary hikes with cash payout
Opposition asks govt to discuss Ram temple donation 'theft', ethanol, Manipur in Parliament
articles.description: Opposition leaders requested discussions on key issues during the upcoming Parliament session. They specifically highlighted the alleged theft of donations at the Ram Temple. Student concerns and the situation in Manipur were also raised as important topics.

articles.title: Nigerian children pay the price for Iran war as malnutrition, poverty surge
articles.description: Children relapsing into malnutrition have become an increasingly common occurrence in the state and across northern Nigeria, according to local health and aid workers, who cite knock-on effects from the Iran war

articles.title: Users of Meta's Facebook, Instagram report suffering some outages
articles.description: Social media platforms Instagram and Facebook experienced widespread outages on Sunday. Users in the United States reported significant issues accessing both the app and website. Downdetector data showed thousands of reports filed by affected users. Access to these popular platforms was also intermittent in Singapore. Meta has not yet provided an immediate comment on the disruptions.

articles.title: NCPI to chalk out Monsoon Session strategy today after TMC rebels' merger
articles.description: The Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI) will hold its parliamentary party meeting on Sunday to finalise its strategy for the Monsoon Session of Parliament, days after 20 rebel Trinamool Congress MPs declared their merger with the party amid a row over their recognition.

articles.title: Manu Joseph: Christopher Nolan's fame precedes his movies but why is the maker of 'The Odyssey' so famous?
articles.description: The British director of blockbusters such as 'Inception' and 'Interstellar' isn’t just talented. Part of the reason his films have gained a cult following is that they are abstract enough for people to see what they want in his work. Does that qualify it as arthouse cinema?

articles.description: The DMK party seeks a clear formula for the women's reservation bill. They want the quota based on the Lok Sabha's current strength. The party also requested clarity on the delimitation exercise. This exercise should not negatively impact southern states, they stated. If affected, southern states should be exempt for twenty-five years.

articles.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah reviewed West Bengal's law and order situation with state officials. He also inspected security measures along the India-Bangladesh border in north Bengal. Shah inaugurated development projects worth Rs 77.06 crore for the Border Security Force. He also reviewed advanced border security technologies and laid foundations for new projects. The meeting followed the passage of a new state act to control anti-social activities.

articles.title: A weak monsoon is not as devastating as it was in the past—but India must not drop its guard
articles.description: India’s farm output has grown more resilient to rainfall deficiencies but this relief is only relative. Inadequate rainfall could still stoke food inflation and compress rural incomes. Let's not drop our guard.

articles.title: Why scientists watched a gene hop from a predator into its prey
articles.description: Jumping genes are responsible for the spread of antibiotic resistance and in the development of many cancers. Scientists are also studying circular RNA molecules, like the one derived from the jumping intron, as next generation vaccine platforms. Understanding jumping genes can thus have profound implications for medicine

articles.title: Rajeev Chandrasekhar accuses CPI(M), IUML of spreading fear over Waqf Act
articles.description: Kerala BJP president Rajeev Chandrasekhar has accused the CPI(M) and the IUML of spreading "fear and misinformation" over the amended Waqf law, asserting that the issue concerns the rule of law rather than religion. His remarks come after the Kerala High Court observed that the State Waqf Board did not comply with the amended law requiring the inclusion of two non-Muslim members.

articles.title: Survival of the wittiest? Scientists debate if wit helped evolve language
articles.description: By identifying ‘living fossils’ in modern speech, some researchers are arguing that human language evolved slowly through a ‘survival of the wittiest’. The theory suggests verbal humour was sexually selected over physical fighting, using visual brain regions to build the foundation of complex modern grammar

articles.title: Why engineering evolution is a high-stakes race between cure and chaos
articles.description: The safest aircraft is one that does not take off. In their letter, the Congress leaders called for a probe into the handling of donations, including cash, gold and silver, and urged that the Trust's accounts and the findings of the investigation be made public.

articles.description: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.

articles.title: Byju's-Aakash settlement offers 30% stake, not binding yet
articles.description: Under the proposed arrangement, Think & Learn would get the stake in exchange for withdrawal of litigation initiated by the company and its lenders against Aakash and Manipal, they said. Reuters earlier reported that the lenders were seeking an about 30% stake in Aakash and that legal action against Byju’s founder, Byju Raveendran, would be dropped.

articles.title: Tata Electronics cyberattack: Mandiant recommendations implemented to prevent future breaches
articles.description: On June 23 ransomware group World Leaks posted more than 200,000 files to the dark web, including details on projects related to some of the company’s global clients, which include Apple and Tesla. Three days later, it said that the company had launched a probe and had tightened internal security protocols.

articles.title: ISM 2.0 puts India’s chip supply chain in spotlight
articles.description: Unlike the first phase, which focused largely on attracting fabs, assembly-test-marking-packaging units and outsourced semiconductor assembly and test facilities, the new phase specifically extends support to parts of the supply chain that sit around chip plants, including semiconductor machinery, equipment, specialty materials, chemicals and gases.

articles.title: US-Iran War News Highlights: US military launches new airstrikes against Iran for deaths of US troops
‘Willful negligence’: Trump threatens Canada with tariff hike over wildfire smoke
India’s Parliament must not stage its Cadaver Synod
articles.description: Parliament must avoid a purposeless exercise over the Justice Yashwant Varma report

articles.title: Oceansat-3A likely to be launched by year-end, says NRSC Director
articles.description: Coastal observation research laboratory inaugurated at Andhra University’s Centre for Studies on Bay of Bengal

articles.title: A trade deal that tests India’s competitive confidence
articles.description: Trade agreements reward nations ready to compete

articles.title: ​Promise of chips: On the India Semiconductor Mission phase II
articles.description: Chipmaking is more than a seasonal obsession in India now

articles.title: ​Battling drug abuse: On Kerala’s Operation Toofan
articles.description: Kerala has shown initiative in coordinated action against cartels

articles.title: Karnataka tops India with 41% share in software exports
articles.description: Karnataka ranks first in the country by contributing 41% share to India's total software exports. Additionally, the state has maintained its top position by securing a 24% share in the nation's overall export sector. In terms of merchandise exports, the state holds the fourth position with a 7.78% share, following Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Tamil Nadu, it said.

articles.title: Letters to The Editor — July 18, 2026
US judge won't block Meta from laying off workers who filed AI discrimination lawsuit
articles.description: ​​District Judge William Orrick in Oakland, California, in a written order said he would not stop Meta from carrying out the layoffs beginning July 22 while the merits of the workers' novel legal claims are decided in private arbitration. ​​The lawsuit filed on Monday claims that in selecting jobs to cut, Meta relied on AI tools that measured productivity and AI token usage, disadvantaging people who missed work because of medical conditions or to care for family members.

articles.title: Apple in early settlement talks with US DOJ over antitrust case, Bloomberg News reports
articles.description: Apple is reportedly in early settlement discussions with the U.S. Department of Justice regarding the ongoing antitrust case. This news, as reported by Bloomberg, suggests a potential resolution to the legal battle between the tech giant and the government over alleged anti-competitive practices.

articles.title: Letting the right hand know that the left can do it too
articles.description: A combination of nature and nurture may work in favour of the ‘freak’

articles.title: Setu chief business officer Anand Raisinghani steps down
articles.description: Anand Raisinghani has resigned from his role at Pine Labs-owned Setu, an API infrastructure company. He is stepping down to pursue new career opportunities outside the organisation. Raisinghani joined Setu in April 2025 after a long career in technology. This departure follows cofounder Nikhil Kumar's exit from daily operations.

articles.title: Meta, Anthropic in talks for potential $10 billion compute lease deal: NYT
articles.description: Meta Platforms is reportedly discussing a significant deal to lease computing power to Anthropic. This potential agreement could be valued at as much as ten billion dollars over two years. Such a partnership would help Meta diversify its revenue streams beyond advertising. Anthropic proposed this deal in June, and Meta is currently considering the terms. Both companies retain the option to exit the agreement early if needed.

articles.title: How New Zealand is protecting some of the world’s rarest wildlife
articles.description: From kiwi and kākāpō to glow worms and little blue penguins, discover how Māori communities, scientists and conservationists are working together to protect New Zealand’s extraordinary endemic wildlife

articles.title: West Asia crisis, uncertain monsoon major risks for growth: RBI Governor
Walmart names Kyle Kinnard to replace departing US COO Kieran Shanahan
articles.description: Walmart has appointed international COO Kyle Kinnard as the new COO of its U.S. operations, replacing Kieran Shanahan, as CEO John Furner continues a broader leadership reshuffle. The move comes amid the retailer's technology-focused transformation, executive changes, workforce cuts, and efforts to navigate softer consumer spending.

articles.title: Mexico earthquake: 7.4 magnitude quake rocks Guatemala, El Salvador
HCLTech opens technology centre in GIFT City; employs 500 engineers
articles.description: The centre will deliver AI-led solutions to the company’s global clients in the financial services industry

articles.title: Scientists confirm atmosphere on potentially habitable planet 49 light-years from Earth
Science Snapshots: July 19, 2026
articles.description: Three recent important research developments, all readable in one minute

articles.title: Pakistan kills 24 Taliban, BLA militants in raids near Afghan border after suicide attack
Joy and agony of living alone
articles.description: It is a transformative experience, teaching you not only independence but also resilience

articles.title: Why does our body scent change distinctly as we age? Are there ways to manage it?
Zepto IPO anchor book nears closure; Norges, Motilal Oswal may take 40-45%
articles.description: In a notable financial move, major investors like Norges and Motilal Oswal are expressing a keen interest in Zepto's upcoming initial public offering, which is valued at approximately Rs 11,000 crore. The anchor book is approaching its closure, with these investors poised to capture a substantial share. Zepto's valuation for this IPO stands at an impressive $5.1 billion, aligning with the burgeoning growth of India's quick commerce sector.

articles.title: Apple overtakes Nvidia to become world’s most valuable company. Here’s how
Karnataka eyes Salesforce partnership to boost AI skilling, e-governance
articles.description: Karnataka is exploring a partnership with Salesforce to boost AI skilling and technology governance, said state IT/BT minister Priyank Kharge. The discussions come as Karnataka steps up engagement with global AI and technology companies to strengthen its digital ecosystem. Salesforce recently partnered the Tripura government to deploy AI-enabled citizen services, an agreement announced at the state’s business conclave in July.

articles.title: Who is Andy Burnham? Britain’s next PM, wife, children and why he’s called the ‘King of the North’
India’s first privately developed orbital-class rocket Vikram-1 set for July 18 lift-off
articles.description: Private space launch company Skyroot Aerospace on July 16, 2026 announced that it will attempt to launch Vikram-1 from the space port’s first launch pad at 11.30 a.m..

articles.title: Google renames NotebookLM as Gemini Notebook, expands AI research features
SpaceX aborts Starship Flight 13 launch seconds before liftoff, targets retry next week
Iran asks Yemen’s Houthis to prepare Red Sea blockade: What it means for global oil
Pema Khandu writes: Ten lessons from 10 years in the Land of the Rising Sun
Apple unseats Nvidia to become world's most valuable company as AI bets shift
articles.description: Apple was last valued at $4.88 trillion as its shares held steady, while Nvidia was roughly at $4.86 ​trillion, following a 3.5% decline. The shift in the pecking order illustrates that investors are broadening their focus beyond the most obvious beneficiaries of the AI boom, such as Nvidia, which had been at the helm for nearly a year. Apple is reclaiming the top spot for the first time since April last year.

articles.title: Zoox recalls self-driving cars because they may not detect smoke
articles.description: Last ​week, ⁠the top U.S. auto ​safety official said self-driving ​car companies must quickly ‌address a "clear pattern" of driverless ​vehicles ​interfering ⁠with law enforcement and other first responders.

articles.title: India now has the funds, the talent and the opening for a research leap
Netflix tumbles as slowing growth, less viewership data spook investors
articles.description: Netflix shares dropped significantly after forecasting slower revenue gains. The company also reduced its viewership data reporting frequency. This move fuels investor concerns about its industry-leading growth potentially peaking. Analysts noted a weaker content lineup this year could impact performance. The streaming giant faces increasing competition from traditional media and YouTube.

articles.title: Opinion | Sonakshi Sinha is brave to speak up for Sonam Wangchuk and CJP. She shouldn’t have to be
A power verdict with consequences beyond Railways
The India-UK CETA is finally in force—now it’s for businesses in both countries to make the most of it
articles.description: The historic pact inked last year by New Delhi and London went into effect this week. Besides a tariff-reduction trade boost, the deal has plenty of avenues for business gains on both sides. Some duty rollbacks are gradual, but their calendar bears no uncertainty.

articles.title: TSMC projects mass production of advanced A14 chips by 2028
articles.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) announced that commercial production of its highly anticipated A14 process is slated to begin in 2028. According to a news report by Focus Taiwan, the announcement signals the next major leap in advanced semiconductor manufacturing as the global chip supply chain races to meet intensifying technological demands.

articles.title: Satluj silences: The answer to this film, is another film
Looking for love, finding fraud: How AI makes matrimony scams harder to spot
Robot dog adapts to terrain without human control, leaps and climbs stairs
Decolonisation cannot begin by pretending English is foreign
India’s mass-market mobile segment declined 45% YoY
articles.description: Overall, India’s smartphone shipments witnessed biggest decline in six years between April to June with shipments going down by 10% during Q2 2026

articles.title: Denying Backward Classes benefits to converts to Islam is unconstitutional
articles.description: Madras High Court ruling denies Backward Class status to converts to Islam, raising constitutional concerns and legal inconsistencies

articles.title: How a dispute over ecommerce firm Coupang is testing US-South Korea ties
articles.description: South Korea's fine on ecommerce firm Coupang has drawn significant US criticism. This dispute raises concerns about Seoul's openness to American technology companies. The issue is impacting broader bilateral relations and security negotiations between the two nations. South Korean officials maintain the fine was for a data leak, not discrimination. Both sides seek a constructive resolution to the ongoing disagreement.

articles.title: X cracks down on content theft, AI now spots copied posts three times faster
Beyond the blackboard: India needs a fluid education policy in the age of AI
articles.description: A structural recalibration is needed in favour of a curriculum with modular learning blocks that evolve dynamically. India has a vast demographic opportunity, but to maximize its value, we must close the gap between policy intent and classroom reality.

articles.title: Meghalaya Assembly to move resolution against uranium mining
articles.description: Various groups have been mounting pressure on the Conrad K. Sangma government after Minister of State for Atomic Energy Jitendra Singh said that uranium mining in Meghalaya is still under examination

articles.title: Google required to open up to AI, search engine rivals under EU-mandated changes
Moonshot AI unveils Kimi K3, the world’s largest open-weight AI model: What to know
Google will have to open up Android and Search data to rivals under new EU specifications
articles.description: Google has consistently opposed these proposed measures, with lawyer Kent Walker claiming that the latest decisions by the regulator come with privacy and security risks

articles.title: The slow erosion of press freedoms
articles.description: Licensing regimes, strategic lawsuits, algorithmic suppression and the slow desiccation of local newsrooms have led to a procedural rather than violent decline

articles.title: How the new U.S. visa rules affect your online privacy | Explained
articles.description: Privacy experts have slammed the U.S. government’s new social media vetting requirements, likening the country’s approach to that of an authoritarian regime. However, they note it is difficult to claim that privacy laws are being broken as visa applicants can choose to ignore the request, though they put their visa application at risk by doing so

articles.title: India smartphone market falls 10% in Q2 2026 as rising prices hit demand
RBI governor Malhotra flags West Asia, weak monsoon as biggest risks to Indian economy
articles.description: The governor said the rupee remains relatively stable despite a stronger dollar, while citing services exports, remittances and FDI as external-sector buffers.

articles.title: Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike: A look at India's longest fasts since 1947 – Irom Sharmila to GD Agrawal
articles.description: India has a history of hunger strikes before and after Independence. Mahatma Gandhi was on a hunger strike for 21 days in 1933. Bhagat Singh for 116 days in 1929. &nbsp;Mamata Banerjee sat on a 25-day-long hunger strike in 2006.

articles.title: ‘Revenge’ still hangs over the Argentina-England rivalry
SpaceX abruptly scrubs Starship test flight
articles.description: The flight was the first mission planned by Elon Musk after SpaceX went public on Wall Street in June, with a record initial public offering of $135

articles.title: Power grids face unexpected slow-burn threat from solar storms
articles.description: Instead of briefly and intensely spiking the strength of currents in a New Zealand power grid, a geomagnetic storm in 2015 was found to have ramped up induced currents over 90 minutes

articles.title: What is Kimi K3, China’s first open AI model to reach 2.8 trillion parameters?
articles.description: Moonshot AI said that Kimi K3 was still behind models such as Claude Fable 5 and GPT 5.6 Sol, but hailed its capacity for coding, knowledge work, and multi-media creation

articles.title: How NASA's Webb discovered giant new exoplanet near Beta Pictoris system
articles.description: According to NASA, Beta Pic d is likely twice the mass of Jupiter, and the smallest of the three known giant planets in this system.
